name: Hannah
diagnosis: Cerebral palsy, epilepsy, KCNQ2, Lennox-Gastaut Syndrome, non-verbal
bio:
Hannah’s story begins with her mother’s prayer—the inspiration for her name, which means “God’s grace.” Her family lovingly calls her “Grace Grace.” Hannah’s joy is unmistakable, with a radiant smile and infectious laugh that bring light everywhere she goes. Proudly calling herself a diva—her van sports a “Diva on Wheels” sign—she arrives in sunglasses, charming everyone. Whether at church, school, dance, or in the community, Hannah leaves a memorable impression.

Hannah’s life is filled with music, laughter, and connection. She enjoys everything from praise songs to pop hits, happily “singing” along to both. She loves flashing lights and beluga whales, often watching them for hours at the aquarium. Hannah is expressive and social—her personality is much bigger than any diagnosis.

Despite all the joy, Hannah and her family faced a serious challenge at home. A bathroom renovated for her accessibility needs was poorly built, resulting in leaks, unsafe floors, and significant water damage. The space became dangerous for both Hannah and her elderly grandparents, who help care for her. Instead of being a safe place, it became a daily source of stress.

That’s when Sunshine on a Ranney Day stepped in. With our experience in accessible design, we care for families like Hannah’s. We completely rebuilt the bathroom. Designer Kianna Patrick created a space that is beautiful and practical. The teal tile and spa-like atmosphere bring peace and comfort to the home. Most importantly, it is safe and reliable. The bathroom is designed to support Hannah’s needs for years to come.

This transformation would not have happened without the generosity of our sponsors and partners. MSI Surfaces provided the beautiful tile that sets the tone for the room. Real Floors Commercial donated labor and installation to bring it all together. Construction Resources gave a lovely vanity. Randall Brothers supplied the framing materials. ServiceWise Electrical handled the electrical work. TKO Plumbing ensured everything worked perfectly. Traditions in Tile & Stone provided the Schluter waterproofing system for a lasting foundation. Sherwin-Williams supplied quality paint, and Georgia Interior Painting added expert finishing touches. Echols Glass & Mirror installed a gold-framed mirror. Top Knobs contributed elegant hardware to complete the design. Bin There Dump That kept the project running smoothly. Nothing Bundt Cakes brought a sweet treat to the celebration with Bundtinis.

Because of this incredible community, Hannah and her family now have a space that reflects her spirit: bright, beautiful, and full of grace.
quote: She is a ray of sunshine and has a spirit that has been described as "sweet as honey."
heroSummary: Hannah received a safe, fully accessible bathroom renovation from Sunshine on a Ranney Day—transforming a hazardous space into a beautiful, spa-like environment that supports her care, comfort, and daily needs.
